reasoning: The failure propagation analysis shows that anomalies on the F1-u interface are preceding and driving symptoms on the F1-c, E2, and A1 interfaces. Specifically, the degradation of the User Plane (F1-u) between the O-CU and O-DU has created a ripple effect, impacting the data flow and triggering subsequent anomalies in the management and control planes (RICs).
actionable_steps: 1. Physical Layer Audit: Perform a health check on the transport network links and hardware interfaces connecting the O-CU and O-DU. 2. Traffic Analysis: Check for sudden bursts of traffic or congestion on the F1-u interface that may be causing buffer overflows and packet drops. 3. Interface Reset: If hardware is healthy, perform a soft reset of the F1-u interface to clear any transient state errors.
